mission.title: Install and Configure Minikube
mission.description: Minikube is a tool that allows you to run a local Kubernetes cluster on your machine. It is ideal for development and testing purposes, providing a simple way to experiment with Kubernetes features without needing a full cloud setup. However, it is not intended for production use and may not replicate the performance or scalability of a managed Kubernetes service.

steps.title: Install Minikube CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Minikube command-line interface, which is necessary to manage your local Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/minikube/releases/v1.38.1/minikube-darwin-amd64

sudo install minikube-darwin-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube

```

> For macOS users; replace 'darwin-amd64' with 'linux-amd64' or 'windows-amd64' as needed.
> This installs the Minikube binary to your PATH.

steps.title: Start Minikube Cluster
steps.description:
This command initializes a new Minikube cluster with a specified Kubernetes version and the number of CPUs and memory allocated.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Adjust the version, CPUs, and memory as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> This sets the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Minikube cluster to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see a single node in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step enables the Kubernetes dashboard and sets up an add-on for monitoring.

```bash

minikube addons enable dashboard

minikube addons enable metrics-server

```

> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for easier management.
> Enables metrics server for resource monitoring.

uninstall.title: Stop and Delete Minikube Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command stops the Minikube cluster and deletes all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

minikube stop

minikube delete

```

> Stops the Minikube cluster.
> Deletes the Minikube cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Minikube
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important data. This step upgrades Minikube to the latest version.

```bash

minikube stop

minikube update-check

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/minikube/releases/v1.38.1/minikube-darwin-amd64 && sudo install minikube-darwin-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube

minikube start

kubectl get nodes

```

> Stops the Minikube cluster.
> Checks for the latest version of Minikube.
> Replace with the latest version as needed.
> Restarts the Minikube cluster with the new version.
> Verify that the node is in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:8443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Minikube cluster may not be running.

**Fix:**
Run 'minikube start' to start the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/darwin/amd64/kubectl' and add it to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'minikube start failed: unable to start VM'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to Minikube.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory allocation using 'minikube start --cpus=4 --memory=8192'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'minikube tunnel: error: unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Minikube cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the cluster is running with 'minikube status' and start it if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Updated to support Kubernetes v1.31, improved performance for multi-cluster setups.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the 'minikube start --vm-driver' flag in favor of 'minikube start --driver'.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.37.x, no specific migration steps are required.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to 'NAME         STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
minikube     Ready    master   5m    v1.31.0' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources available before starting the installation.
